title: Scan a code in inventory to open the matching record
description: This verifies that a scanner input in inventory automatically opens the matching batch or product record.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Scan a valid batch or product code into the focused field
Verify the matching record opens automatically
Verify traceability data for the record is displayed

title: Print a thermal label from an active record
description: This verifies that a user can open an active batch or product, preview a thermal label, and reach the browser print dialog with the correct label layout.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Open an active batch or product record
Verify the browser print dialog is shown with a QR-enabled thermal label layout

title: Scan a QR code in production to load batch traceability
description: This verifies that scanning a printed QR code in production opens the corresponding batch information for traceability.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Scan a valid QR code into the focused field
Verify the corresponding batch information loads

title: Reprint a batch label with a reason and capture audit history
description: This verifies that printing the same batch label twice requires a reprint reason and records both print events in history.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Choose to print the label again
Verify the print history shows an initial print and a reprint
Verify the reprint includes an operator and timestamp record

title: Mark a second label print as a reprint
description: This verifies that printing the same batch label twice records the later action as a reprint after a reason is selected.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Open a batch record from the dashboard
Verify the second print action is recorded as a reprint
Verify the audit history shows both the initial print and the reprint

title: Scan a batch or product from inventory and open its record
description: This verifies that scanning a valid code from inventory opens the matching record and loads traceability details.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Use the keyboard-emulation scanner to scan a valid batch or product QR or barcode

title: Print a completed production order as a clean document
description: This verifies that a completed production order can be previewed and sent to print in a standard document layout without web controls.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Choose to print the order sheet
Verify the browser print dialog is shown for a standard document layout
Verify interactive web controls are absent from the print preview

title: Open a scanned record from the dashboard workflow
description: This verifies that a user can navigate from the dashboard, open a record, and begin the print workflow from the same session.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Open a batch record from the dashboard
Open the print action for the batch
Verify the print workflow is available for the selected record
Verify the selected batch record remains in context

title: Change the default label size and use it for the next label
description: This verifies that an administrator can update the default label template and that subsequent label printing uses the new size.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Select a new default label size
Open an active batch or product record
Verify the label preview uses the updated default template size
Verify QR-enabled label content is still present

title: View a batch label print history
description: This verifies that a user can open a batch record and review prior print actions with the expected audit details.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Open a batch record from the dashboard
Open the print history view for the batch
Verify prior print actions are displayed
Verify the print history includes who printed, when it was printed, the target record, and the print type

title: Print a recipe as a formatted operational document
description: This verifies that a recipe can be opened, previewed, and printed in a clean document layout suitable for offline use.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Verify a clean print-friendly document preview is displayed
Verify interactive web controls are absent from the preview

title: Record the print timestamp and operator for each label print
description: This verifies that each label print is captured in the audit history with its timestamp and operator identity.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Open a batch record from the dashboard
Verify the print history entry is visible
Verify the print history entry includes the operator and timestamp

title: Print an inventory summary in a standard paper layout
description: This verifies that an inventory summary can be opened and printed as a standard operational document.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Verify the browser print dialog is shown for a standard paper document
Verify the summary is presented in a print-friendly layout

title: Update QR error correction settings and generate a label
description: This verifies that an administrator can change QR configuration and later generate a label using the updated settings.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Open an active batch or product record
Verify the label preview reflects the updated QR configuration
Verify the label preview remains printable

title: Block printing for a draft batch
description: This verifies that a draft batch cannot be printed and the unavailable state is shown instead of a printable label.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Verify a printing unavailable state is shown
Verify no label preview is displayed

title: Ignore invalid scanner input without opening a record
description: This verifies that malformed scanner input does not open a record and the user remains on the lookup screen.

steps.description: Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Scan an invalid code into the focused field
Verify the lookup screen remains available
